NRSV.label: New Revised Standard Version
NRSV.rights: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

text.NRSV: Paul and Timothy, servants of Christ Jesus, To all the saints in Christ Jesus who are in Philippi, with the bishops and deacons:
text.Rotherham: Paul and Timothy, servants of Jesus Christ,—unto all the saints in Christ Jesus who are in Philippi, with overseers and ministers:—
text.KJV: 1:1 Paul and Timotheus the seruants of Iesus Christ, to all the Saints in Christ Iesus, which are at Philippi, with the Bishops and Deacons:
text.DRV: 1:1 Paul and Timothee the servants of Jesus Christ; to all the saints in Christ Jesus that are at Philippi, with the Bishops and Deacons.

text.NRSV: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: Favour unto you, and peace, from God our Father and Lord Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: 1:2 Grace be vnto you, and peace, from God our Father, and from the Lord Iesus Christ.
text.DRV: 1:2 Grace to you and peace from God our Father, and our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: I thank my God every time I remember you,
text.Rotherham: I am giving thanks unto my God, on occasion of all my remembrance of you,
text.KJV: 1:3 I thanke my God vpon euery remembrance of you,
text.DRV: 1:3 I give thanks to my God in all memory of you

text.NRSV: constantly in every prayer of mine for all of you, praying with joy
text.Rotherham: At all times, in every supplication of mine, in behalf of you all, with joy, my supplication, making,—
text.KJV: 1:4 Alwayes in euery prayer of mine for you all making request, with ioy
text.DRV: 1:4 (always in all my prayers for all you, with joy making petition)

text.NRSV: because of your sharing in the gospel from the first day until now.
text.Rotherham: On account of your contribution unto the glad-message, from the first day until the present:
text.KJV: 1:5 For your felowship in the Gospel from the first day vntill now;
text.DRV: 1:5 for your communicating in the Gospel of Christ from the first day until now.

text.NRSV: I am confident of this, that the one who began a good work among you will bring it to completion by the day of Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: Being persuaded of this very thing—that, he who hath begun in you a good work, will perfect it, until the day of Jesus Christ;
text.KJV: 1:6 Being confident of this very thing, that he which hath begun a good work in you, will performe it vntil the day of Iesus Christ:
text.DRV: 1:6 trusting this same thing, that he which hath begun in you a good work, will perfit it unto the day of Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: It is right for me to think this way about all of you, because you hold me in your heart, for all of you share in God’s grace with me, both in my imprisonment and in the defense and confirmation of the gospel.
text.Rotherham: According as it is right in me to have this regard in behalf of you all,—because ye have had me in your hearts, both in my bonds and in the defence and confirmation of the glad-message, all of you being, joint partakers of my favour.
text.KJV: 1:7 Euen as it is meete for mee to thinke this of you all, because I haue you in my heart, in as much as both in my bonds, and in the defence and confirmation of the Gospel, ye all are partakers of my grace.
text.DRV: 1:7 As it is reason for me, this to think for all you, for that I have you in heart, & in my bands, and in the defence, and the confirmation of the Gospel, all you to be partakers of my joy.

text.NRSV: For God is my witness, how I long for all of you with the compassion of Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: For God is, my witness, how I long for you all in the tender affections of Christ Jesus.
text.KJV: 1:8 For God is my record, how greatly I long after you all, in the bowels of Iesus Christ.
text.DRV: 1:8 For God is my witness, how I covet you all in the bowels of Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: And this is my prayer, that your love may overflow more and more with knowledge and full insight
text.Rotherham: And, this, I pray—that, your love, may be, yet more and more, pre-eminent in personal knowledge and all perception,
text.KJV: 1:9 And this I pray, that your loue may abound yet more & more in knowledge, and in all iudgment.
text.DRV: 1:9 And this I pray, that your charity may more and more abound in knowledge and in all understanding:

text.NRSV: to help you determine what is best, so that in the day of Christ you may be pure and blameless,
text.Rotherham: To the end ye may be putting to the test the things that differ, in order that ye may be incorrupt and may give no occasion of stumbling, unto the day of Christ,
text.KJV: 1:10 That ye may approue things that are excellent, that ye may be sincere, and without offence till the day of Christ.
text.DRV: 1:10 that you may approve the better things, that you may be sincere and without offence unto the day of Christ,

text.NRSV: having produced the harvest of righteousness that comes through Jesus Christ for the glory and praise of God.
text.Rotherham: Filled with that fruit of righteousness which is through Jesus Christ unto the glory and praise of God.
text.KJV: 1:11 Being filled with the fruites of righteousnesse, which are by Iesus Christ vnto the glory and praise of God.
text.DRV: 1:11 replenished with the fruit of justice by Jesus Christ, unto the glory and praise of God.

text.NRSV: I want you to know, beloved, that what has happened to me has actually helped to spread the gospel,
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, I am minded, brethren, that ye should be, getting to know, that, the things which relate unto myself, rather, unto an advancement of the glad-message, have fallen out,
text.KJV: 1:12 But I would yee should vnderstand brethren, that the things which happened vnto mee, haue fallen out rather vnto the furtherance of the Gospel.
text.DRV: 1:12 And I will have you know, brethren, that the things about me are come to the more furtherance of the Gospel:

text.NRSV: so that it has become known throughout the whole imperial guard and to everyone else that my imprisonment is for Christ;
text.Rotherham: So that, my bonds, have become manifest in Christ, in the whole palace, and unto all the rest,—
text.KJV: 1:13 So that my bonds in Christ, are manifest in all the palace, and in all other places.
text.DRV: 1:13 so that my bands were made manifest in Christ in all the court, and in all the rest,

text.NRSV: and most of the brothers and sisters, having been made confident in the Lord by my imprisonment, dare to speak the word with greater boldness and without fear.
text.Rotherham: And [so that], the most of the brethren in the Lord, assured by my bonds, are becoming more abundantly bold to be fearlessly speaking the word of God:—
text.KJV: 1:14 And many of the brethren in the Lord, waxing confident, by my bonds, are much more bold to speake the word without feare.
text.DRV: 1:14 that many of our brethren in our Lord, having confidence in my bands, were bold more aboundantly without fear to speak the word of God.

text.NRSV: Some proclaim Christ from envy and rivalry, but others from goodwill.
text.Rotherham: Some, indeed, by reason of envy and strife—some, however, by reason of good will—are proclaiming, the Christ:
text.KJV: 1:15 Some in deed preach Christ, euen of enuie and strife, and some also of good will.
text.DRV: 1:15 Some indeed even for envy and contention: but some also for good will preach Christ.

text.NRSV: These proclaim Christ out of love, knowing that I have been put here for the defense of the gospel;
text.Rotherham: These, indeed, out of love, knowing that, for the defence of the glad-message, I am set;
text.KJV: 1:16 The one preach Christ of contention, not syncerely, supposing to adde affliction to my bonds:
text.DRV: 1:16 Some of charity: knowing that I am set unto the defence of the Gospel.

text.NRSV: the others proclaim Christ out of selfish ambition, not sincerely but intending to increase my suffering in my imprisonment.
text.Rotherham: But, those, out of faction, are declaring, the Christ, not purely—supposing to rouse up, tribulation, with my bonds.
text.KJV: 1:17 But the other of loue, knowing that I am set for the defence of the Gospel.
text.DRV: 1:17 And some of contention preach Christ not sincerely: supposing that they raise affliction to my bands.

text.NRSV: What does it matter? Just this, that Christ is proclaimed in every way, whether out of false motives or true; and in that I rejoice. Yes, and I will continue to rejoice,
text.Rotherham: What then? That, in any way, whether in pretext, or in truth, Christ, is declared, and, in this, I rejoice—yea, and will rejoice!
text.KJV: 1:18 What then? Notwithstanding euery way, whether in pretence, or in trueth: Christ is preached, and I therein doe reioyce, yea, and will reioyce.
text.DRV: 1:18 But what? So that by all means, whether by occasion, or by truth, Christ be preached: in this also I rejoice, yea & will rejoice.

text.NRSV: for I know that through your prayers and the help of the Spirit of Jesus Christ this will turn out for my deliverance.
text.Rotherham: For I know that, this, shall turn unto me for salvation—through your supplication, and the supply of the Spirit of Jesus Christ,—
text.KJV: 1:19 For I know that this shall turne to my saluation through your prayer, and the supplie of the spirit of Iesus Christ,
text.DRV: 1:19 For I know that this shall fall out to me unto salvation by your prayer and the subministration of the Spirit of Jesus Christ,

text.NRSV: It is my eager expectation and hope that I will not be put to shame in any way, but that by my speaking with all boldness, Christ will be exalted now as always in my body, whether by life or by death.
text.Rotherham: According to my eager outlook and hope, that, in nothing, shall I be put to shame, but, with all freedom of speech, as always, now also, shall Christ be magnified in my body, whether through means of life, or of death.
text.KJV: 1:20 According to my earnest expectation, and my hope, that in nothing I shalbe ashamed: but that with all boldnes, as alwayes, so now also Christ shal be magnified in my body, whether it be by life or by death.
text.DRV: 1:20 according to my expectation & hope; because in nothing shall I be confounded, but in all confidence as always, now also shall Christ be magnified in my body, whether it be by life, or by death.

text.NRSV: For to me, living is Christ and dying is gain.
text.Rotherham: For, unto me—living, is Christ, and, dying, gain.
text.KJV: 1:21 For to me to liue is Christ, and to die is gaine.
text.DRV: 1:21 For unto me, to live is Christ: and to die is gain.

text.NRSV: If I am to live in the flesh, that means fruitful labor for me; and I do not know which I prefer.
text.Rotherham: But, if living in flesh, [is Christ], this, unto me, is a fruit of work,—and, what I shall choose, I make not known:
text.KJV: 1:22 But if I liue in the flesh, this is the fruit of my labour: yet what I shal chuse, I wote not.
text.DRV: 1:22 And if to live in the flesh, this unto me be the fruit of the work, and what I shall choose I know not.

text.NRSV: I am hard pressed between the two: my desire is to depart and be with Christ, for that is far better;
text.Rotherham: I am held in constraint, however, by reason of the two,—having, the coveting, to be released, and to be with, Christ, for it were far better!
text.KJV: 1:23 For I am in a strait betwixt two, hauing a desire to depart, & to bee with Christ, which is farre better.
text.DRV: 1:23 And I am straitened of the two: having desire to be dissolved & to be with Christ, a thing much more better.

text.NRSV: but to remain in the flesh is more necessary for you.
text.Rotherham: But, to abide still in the flesh, is more needful for your sake;
text.KJV: 1:24 Neuertheles, to abide in the flesh, is more needfull for you.
text.DRV: 1:24 But to abide in the flesh, necessary for you.

text.NRSV: Since I am convinced of this, I know that I will remain and continue with all of you for your progress and joy in faith,
text.Rotherham: And, of, this, being assured, know that I shall abide,—yea abide with you all, for your advancement, and the joy of your faith;
text.KJV: 1:25 And hauing this confidence, I know that I shall abide and continue with you all, for your furtherance and ioy of faith,
text.DRV: 1:25 And trusting this, I know that I shall abide and continue with you all, unto your furtherance and joy of the faith:

text.NRSV: so that I may share abundantly in your boasting in Christ Jesus when I come to you again.
text.Rotherham: In order that, your matter of boasting, may be more abundant in Christ Jesus, regarding me, through my own presence again with you.
text.KJV: 1:26 That your reioycing may bee more abundant in Iesus Christ for me, by my comming to you againe.
text.DRV: 1:26 that your gratulation may abound in Christ Jesus in me, by my coming again to you.

text.NRSV: Only, live your life in a manner worthy of the gospel of Christ, so that, whether I come and see you or am absent and hear about you, I will know that you are standing firm in one spirit, striving side by side with one mind for the faith of the gospel,
text.Rotherham: Only, in a manner worthy of the glad-message of the Christ, be using your citizenship; in order that—whether coming and seeing you, or being absent, I may hear of the things which concern you—that ye are standing fast in one spirit, with one soul, joining for the combat along with the faith of the glad-message;
text.KJV: 1:27 Onely let your conuersation bee as it becommeth the Gospel of Christ, that whether I come and see you, or else be absent, I may heare of your affaires, that yee stand fast in one spirit, with one minde, striuing together for the faith of the Gospel,
text.DRV: 1:27 Only converse ye worthy of the Gospel of Christ: that whether when I come and see you, or else be absent, I may hear of you that you stand in one Spirit, of one mind labouring together to the faith of the Gospel.

text.NRSV: and are in no way intimidated by your opponents. For them this is evidence of their destruction, but of your salvation. And this is God’s doing.
text.Rotherham: And not being affrighted in anything by the opposers,—the which, is, unto them, a token of destruction, although of your salvation, and this from God;
text.KJV: 1:28 And in nothing terrified by your aduersaries, which is to them an euident token of perdition: but to you of saluation, and that of God.
text.DRV: 1:28 And in nothing be ye terrified of the adversaries, which to them is cause of perdition: but to you of salvation, & this of God:

text.NRSV: For he has graciously granted you the privilege not only of believing in Christ, but of suffering for him as well—
text.Rotherham: Because, unto you, hath it been given as a favour, in behalf of Christ, not only, on him, to believe, but also, in his behalf, to suffer:—
text.KJV: 1:29 For vnto you it is giuen in the behalfe of Christ, not onely to beleeue on him, but also to suffer for his sake,
text.DRV: 1:29 for to you it is given for Christ, not only that you believe in him, but also that you suffer for him,

text.NRSV: since you are having the same struggle that you saw I had and now hear that I still have.
text.Rotherham: Having, the same contest, which ye have seen in me, and now hear to be in me.
text.KJV: 1:30 Hauing the same conflict which ye saw in me, and now heare to be in me.
text.DRV: 1:30 having the same combat like as you have seen in me, and now have heard of me.

text.NRSV: If then there is any encouragement in Christ, any consolation from love, any sharing in the Spirit, any compassion and sympathy,
text.Rotherham: If there be, therefore, any encouragement in Christ, if any comfort of love, if any fellowship of spirit, if any tender affections and compassions,
text.KJV: 2:1 If there bee therefore any consolation in Christ, if any comfort of loue, if any fellowship of the Spirit, if any bowels, & mercies;
text.DRV: 2:1 IF therefore there be any consolation in Christ, if any solace of charity, if any society of spirit, if any bowels of commiseration;

text.NRSV: make my joy complete: be of the same mind, having the same love, being in full accord and of one mind.
text.Rotherham: Fill ye up my joy—that, the same thing, ye esteem, the same love, possessing, joined in soul, the one thing, esteeming,—
text.KJV: 2:2 Fulfill ye my ioy, that yee be like minded, hauing the same loue, being of one accord, of one minde.
text.DRV: 2:2 fulfill my joy, that you be of one meaning, having the same charity, of one mind, agreeing in one.

text.NRSV: Do nothing from selfish ambition or conceit, but in humility regard others as better than yourselves.
text.Rotherham: Nothing by way of faction, nothing by way of vain-glory,—but, in lowliness of mind, accounting, one another, superior to yourselves,—
text.KJV: 2:3 Let nothing bee done through strife, or vaine glory, but in lowlinesse of minde let each esteeme other better then themselues.
text.DRV: 2:3 Nothing by contention, neither by vain glory: but in humility, each counting other better than themselves:

text.NRSV: Let each of you look not to your own interests, but to the interests of others.
text.Rotherham: Not, to your own things, severally looking, but, to the things of others, severally:—
text.KJV: 2:4 Looke not euery man on his owne things, but euery man also on the things of others.
text.DRV: 2:4 every one not considering the things that are their own, but those that are other men's.

text.NRSV: Let the same mind be in you that was in Christ Jesus,
text.Rotherham: The same thing, esteem, in yourselves, which also, in Christ Jesus, [ye esteem],—
text.KJV: 2:5 Let this minde bee in you, which was also in Christ Iesus:
text.DRV: 2:5 For this think in yourselves, which also in Christ Jesus;

text.NRSV: who, though he was in the form of God, did not regard equality with God as something to be exploited,
text.Rotherham: Who, in form of God, subsisting, not, a thing to be seized, accounted the being equal with God,
text.KJV: 2:6 Who being in the forme of God, thought it not robbery to bee equall with God:
text.DRV: 2:6 who when he was in the form of God, thought it no robbery, himself to be equal to God:

text.NRSV: but emptied himself, taking the form of a slave, being born in human likeness. And being found in human form,
text.Rotherham: But, himself, emptied, taking, a servant’s form, coming to be, in men’s likeness;
text.KJV: 2:7 But made himselfe of no reputation, and tooke vpon him the forme of a seruant, and was made in the likenesse of men.
text.DRV: 2:7 but he exinanited himself, taking the form of a servant, made into the similitude of men, and in shape found as a man.

text.NRSV: he humbled himself and became obedient to the point of death— even death on a cross.
text.Rotherham: And, in fashion, being found, as a man, humbled himself, becoming obedient as far as death, yea, death upon a cross.
text.KJV: 2:8 And being found in fashion as a man, he humbled himselfe, and became obedient vnto death, euen the death of the Crosse.
text.DRV: 2:8 He humbled himself, made obedient unto death: even the death of the cross.

text.NRSV: Therefore God also highly exalted him and gave him the name that is above every name,
text.Rotherham: Wherefore also, God, uplifted him far on high, and favoured him with the name which is above every name,—
text.KJV: 2:9 Wherefore God also hath highly exalted him, and giuen him a Name which is aboue euery name:
text.DRV: 2:9 For the which thing God also hath exalted him, and hath given him a name which is above all names:

text.NRSV: so that at the name of Jesus every knee should bend, in heaven and on earth and under the earth,
text.Rotherham: In order that, in the name of Jesus, every knee might bow—of beings in heaven, and on earth, and underground,—
text.KJV: 2:10 That at the Name of Iesus euery knee should bow, of things in heauen, and things in earth, and things vnder the earth:
text.DRV: 2:10 that in the name of Jesus every knee bow of the celestials, terrestrials, and infernals:

text.NRSV: and every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.
text.Rotherham: And, every tongue, might openly confess—that Jesus Christ is, Lord, unto the glory of God the Father.
text.KJV: 2:11 And that euery tongue should confesse, that Iesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.
text.DRV: 2:11 and every tongue confess that our Lord Jesus Christ is in the glory of God the Father.

text.NRSV: Therefore, my beloved, just as you have always obeyed me, not only in my presence, but much more now in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling;
text.Rotherham: So, then, my beloved—even as ye have always obeyed, not [as] in my presence only, but, now, much more, in my absence, with fear and trembling, your own salvation, be working out;
text.KJV: 2:12 Wherefore, my beloued, as yee haue alwayes obeyed, not as in my presence onely, but now much more in my absence; worke out your owne saluation with feare, and trembling.
text.DRV: 2:12 Therefore, my Dearest, (as you have always obeyed) not as in the presence of me only, but much more now in my absence, with fear and trembling work your salvation.

text.NRSV: for it is God who is at work in you, enabling you both to will and to work for his good pleasure.
text.Rotherham: For it is, God, who energiseth within you, both the desiring and the energising, in behalf of his good pleasure.
text.KJV: 2:13 For it is God which worketh in you, both to will, and to doe, of his good pleasure.
text.DRV: 2:13 For it is God that worketh in you both to will and to accomplish, according to his good will.

text.NRSV: Do all things without murmuring and arguing,
text.Rotherham: All things, be doing, apart from murmurings and disputings;
text.KJV: 2:14 Doe all things without murmurings, and disputings:
text.DRV: 2:14 And do ye all things without murmurings and staggerings:

text.NRSV: so that you may be blameless and innocent, children of God without blemish in the midst of a crooked and perverse generation, in which you shine like stars in the world.
text.Rotherham: In order that ye may become faultless and inviolate, children of God, blameless amidst a crooked and perverted generation, amongst whom ye appear as luminaries in the world,
text.KJV: 2:15 That yee may bee blamelesse and harmelesse, the sonnes of God, without rebuke, in the middes of a crooked and peruerse nation, among whom ye shine as lights in the world:
text.DRV: 2:15 that you may be without blame, and the simple children of God, without reprehension in the midst of a crooked and perverse Generation. Among whom you shine as lights in the world:

text.NRSV: It is by your holding fast to the word of life that I can boast on the day of Christ that I did not run in vain or labor in vain.
text.Rotherham: A word of light, holding forth,—as a matter of boasting unto me, for the day of Christ,—that, not in vain, I ran, nor, in vain, I toiled:—
text.KJV: 2:16 Holding foorth the word of life, that I may reioyce in the day of Christ, that I haue not runne in vaine, neither laboured in vaine.
text.DRV: 2:16 containing the word of life to my glory in the day of Christ, because I have not run in vain, nor in vain laboured.

text.NRSV: But even if I am being poured out as a libation over the sacrifice and the offering of your faith, I am glad and rejoice with all of you—
text.Rotherham: Nay! if I am even to be poured out as a drink-offering, upon the sacrifice and public ministry of your faith, I rejoice, yea rejoice together with you all,—
text.KJV: 2:17 Yea, and if I bee offered vpon the sacrifice and seruice of your faith, I ioy, and reioyce with you all.
text.DRV: 2:17 But and if I be immolated, upon the Sacrifice and service of your faith, I rejoice and congratulate with you all.

text.NRSV: and in the same way you also must be glad and rejoice with me.
text.Rotherham: For the same cause, moreover, do, ye also, rejoice, yea rejoice together with me.
text.KJV: 2:18 For the same cause also doe ye ioy, and reioyce with me.
text.DRV: 2:18 And the self-same thing do you also rejoice, and congratulate with me.

text.NRSV: I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon, so that I may be cheered by news of you.
text.Rotherham: I am hoping, however, in the Lord Jesus—Timothy, shortly, to send unto you, in order that, I also, may be of cheerful soul, when I have ascertained the things that concern you.
text.KJV: 2:19 But I trust in the Lord Iesus, to send Timotheus shortly vnto you, that I also may bee of good comfort, when I know your state.
text.DRV: 2:19 And I hope in our Lord Jesus, to send Timothee unto you quickly, that I also may be of good comfort, when I know the things pertaining to you.

text.NRSV: I have no one like him who will be genuinely concerned for your welfare.
text.Rotherham: For, no one, have I, of equal soul, who, genuinely, as to the things that concern you, will be anxious;
text.KJV: 2:20 For I haue no man like minded, who will naturally care for your state.
text.DRV: 2:20 For I have no man so of one mind that with sincere affection is careful for you.

text.NRSV: All of them are seeking their own interests, not those of Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: For, they all, their own things, do seek, not the things of Christ Jesus;
text.KJV: 2:21 For all seeke their owne, not the things which are Iesus Christs.
text.DRV: 2:21 For all seek the things that are their own; not the things that are Jesus Christ's.

text.NRSV: But Timothy’s worth you know, how like a son with a father he has served with me in the work of the gospel.
text.Rotherham: But, of the proof of him, be taking note,—that, as child, with father, with me, hath he done service for the glad-message;
text.KJV: 2:22 But ye know the proofe of him, That as a sonne with the father, hee hath serued with me, in the Gospel.
text.DRV: 2:22 And know ye an experiment of him, that as a son the Father, so hath he served with me in the Gospel.

text.NRSV: I hope therefore to send him as soon as I see how things go with me;
text.Rotherham: Him, indeed, therefore, am I hoping to send—as soon as I can look off from the things that concern myself,—forthwith.
text.KJV: 2:23 Him therefore I hope to send presently, so soone as I shall see how it wil goe with me.
text.DRV: 2:23 This man therefore I hope to send unto you, immediately as I shall see the things that concern me.

text.NRSV: and I trust in the Lord that I will also come soon.
text.Rotherham: I am assured, however, in the Lord,—that I, myself, shall shortly come.
text.KJV: 2:24 But I trust in the Lord, that I also my selfe shall come shortly.
text.DRV: 2:24 And I trust in our Lord that myself also shall come to you quickly.

text.NRSV: Still, I think it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus—my brother and co-worker and fellow soldier, your messenger and minister to my need;
text.Rotherham: Needful, nevertheless have I accounted it—Epaphroditus, my brother and fellow worker and fellow soldier, but your apostle and public minister to my need, to send unto you;
text.KJV: 2:25 Yet I supposed it necessary, to send to you Epaphroditus my brother and companion in labour, and fellow souldiour, but your messenger, and hee that ministred to my wants.
text.DRV: 2:25 But I have thought it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus my brother and coadjutor and fellow-soldier, but your Apostle, and minister of my necessity.

text.NRSV: for he has been longing for all of you, and has been distressed because you heard that he was ill.
text.Rotherham: Since he hath been longing to see, you all, and hath been in great distress, because ye had heard he was sick;—
text.KJV: 2:26 For hee longed after you all, and was full of heauinesse, because that yee had heard that he had bene sicke.
text.DRV: 2:26 Because indeed he had a desire toward you all: and was pensive, for that you had heard that he was sick.

text.NRSV: He was indeed so ill that he nearly died. But God had mercy on him, and not only on him but on me also, so that I would not have one sorrow after another.
text.Rotherham: And, in fact, he was sick, nigh unto death; but, God, had mercy on him,—and, not on him only, but, on me also, lest, sorrow upon sorrow, I should have.
text.KJV: 2:27 For indeed he was sicke nigh vnto death, but God had mercy on him: and not on him onely, but on mee also, lest I should haue sorow vpon sorow.
text.DRV: 2:27 For indeed he was sick even to death: but God had mercy on him: and not only on him, but on me also, lest I should have sorrow upon sorrow.

text.NRSV: I am the more eager to send him, therefore, in order that you may rejoice at seeing him again, and that I may be less anxious.
text.Rotherham: The more promptly, therefore, have I sent him, that, seeing him again, ye may rejoice, and, I, the less sorrowful, may be.
text.KJV: 2:28 I sent him therefore the more carefully, that when ye see him againe, ye may reioyce, and that I may bee the lesse sorrowfull.
text.DRV: 2:28 Therefore I sent him the more speedily: that seeing him, you may rejoice again, and I may be without sorrow.

text.NRSV: Welcome him then in the Lord with all joy, and honor such people,
text.Rotherham: Be giving him welcome, therefore, in the Lord, with all joy; and, such as he, in honour, be holding,—
text.KJV: 2:29 Receiue him therfore in the Lord with all gladnesse, and hold such in reputation:
text.DRV: 2:29 Receive him therefore with all joy in our Lord: and such entreat with honour.

text.NRSV: because he came close to death for the work of Christ, risking his life to make up for those services that you could not give me.
text.Rotherham: Because, for the sake of the work of the Lord, unto death, he drew nigh, running hazard with his life, that he might fill up your lack of the public service, towards me.
text.KJV: 2:30 Because for the worke of Christ he was nigh vnto death, not regarding his life, to supply your lacke of seruice toward me.
text.DRV: 2:30 because for the work of Christ, he came to the point of death: yielding his life, that he might fulfill that which on your part wanted toward my service.

text.NRSV: Finally, my brothers and sisters, rejoice in the Lord. To write the same things to you is not troublesome to me, and for you it is a safeguard.
text.Rotherham: For the rest, my brethren—rejoice in the Lord. To be writing, the same things, unto you, to me, is not irksome, while, for you, is safe:—
text.KJV: 3:1 Finally, my brethren, reioyce in the Lorde. To write the same things to you, to me indeed is not grieuous: but for you it is safe.
text.DRV: 3:1 From hence-forth, my brethren, rejoice in our Lord. To write the same things unto you, to me surely it is not tedious, and to you it is necessary.

text.NRSV: Beware of the dogs, beware of the evil workers, beware of those who mutilate the flesh!
text.Rotherham: Beware of the dogs, beware of mischievous workers, beware of the mutilation;
text.KJV: 3:2 Beware of dogs, beware of euill workers: beware of the concision.
text.DRV: 3:2 See the dogs, see the evil workers, see the concision.

text.NRSV: For it is we who are the circumcision, who worship in the Spirit of God and boast in Christ Jesus and have no confidence in the flesh—
text.Rotherham: For, we, are the circumcision, who in the Spirit of God, are doing divine service, and are boasting in Christ Jesus, and, not in flesh, having confidence,—
text.KJV: 3:3 For we are the circumcision, which worship God in the spirit, and reioyce in Christ Iesus, and haue no confidence in the flesh.
text.DRV: 3:3 For we are the circumcision, which in spirit serve God: and we glory in Christ Jesus, and not having confidence in the flesh,

text.NRSV: even though I, too, have reason for confidence in the flesh. If anyone else has reason to be confident in the flesh, I have more:
text.Rotherham: Although, indeed, I, might have confidence even in flesh. If any other thinketh to have confidence in flesh, I, more:—
text.KJV: 3:4 Though I might also haue confidence in the flesh. If any other man thinketh that hee hath whereof hee might trust in the flesh, I more:
text.DRV: 3:4 albeit I also have confidence in the flesh. If any other man seem to have confidence in the flesh, I more,

text.NRSV: circumcised on the eighth day, a member of the people of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew born of Hebrews; as to the law, a Pharisee;
text.Rotherham: Circumcised, the eighth day, of the race of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew of Hebrews,—regarding law, a Pharisee,
text.KJV: 3:5 Circumcised the eight day, of the stocke of Israel, of the tribe of Beniamin, an Hebrew of the Hebrewes, as touching the Law, a Pharise:
text.DRV: 3:5 circumcised the eighth day, of the stock of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, an Hebrew of Hebrews: according to the Law, a Pharisee:

text.NRSV: as to zeal, a persecutor of the church; as to righteousness under the law, blameless.
text.Rotherham: Regarding zeal, persecuting the assembly, regarding the righteousness that is in law, having become blameless.
text.KJV: 3:6 Concerning zeale, persecuting the Church: touching the righteousnesse which is in the Law, blamelesse.
text.DRV: 3:6 according to emulation, persecuting the Church of God: according to the justice that is in the Law, conversing without blame.

text.NRSV: Yet whatever gains I had, these I have come to regard as loss because of Christ.
text.Rotherham: But, whatever things, unto me, were gain, the same, have I accounted, for the Christ’s sake, loss;
text.KJV: 3:7 But what things were gaine to me, those I counted losse for Christ.
text.DRV: 3:7 But the things that were gains to me, those have I esteemed for Christ, detriments.

text.NRSV: More than that, I regard everything as loss because of the surpassing value of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord. For his sake I have suffered the loss of all things, and I regard them as rubbish, in order that I may gain Christ
text.Rotherham: Yea, doubtless! and I account all things to be, loss, because of the excellency of the knowledge of Christ Jesus my Lord, for the sake of whom, the loss, of all things, have I suffered, and do account them refuse, in order that, Christ, I may win,
text.KJV: 3:8 Yea doubtlesse, and I count all things but losse, for the excellencie of the knowledge of Christ Iesus my Lord: for whom I haue suffered the losse of all things, and doe count them but doung, that I may win Christ,
text.DRV: 3:8 Yea but I esteem all things to be detriment for the passing knowledge of Jesus Christ my Lord: for whom I have made all things as detriment, and do esteem them as dung, that I may gain Christ:

text.NRSV: and be found in him, not having a righteousness of my own that comes from the law, but one that comes through faith in Christ, the righteousness from God based on faith.
text.Rotherham: And be found in him—not having a righteousness of my own, that which is by law, but that which is through faith in Christ, the righteousness which is, of God, upon my faith,—
text.KJV: 3:9 And be found in him, not hauing mine owne righteousnesse, which is of the Law, but that which is through the faith of Christ, the righteousnesse which is of God by faith:
text.DRV: 3:9 and may be found in him not having my justice which is of the Law, but that which is of the faith of Christ, which is of God, justice in faith:

text.NRSV: I want to know Christ and the power of his resurrection and the sharing of his sufferings by becoming like him in his death,
text.Rotherham: To get to know him, and the power of his resurrection and fellowship of his sufferings, becoming conformed unto his death,—
text.KJV: 3:10 That I may know him, and the power of his resurrection, and the fellowship of his sufferings, being made conformable vnto his death,
text.DRV: 3:10 to know him, and the virtue of his resurrection, and the society of his passions, configured to his death,

text.NRSV: if somehow I may attain the resurrection from the dead.
text.Rotherham: If by any means I may advance to the earlier resurrection, which is from among the dead:
text.KJV: 3:11 If by any meanes I might attaine vnto the resurrection of the dead.
text.DRV: 3:11 if by any means I may come to the resurrection which is from the dead.

text.NRSV: Not that I have already obtained this or have already reached the goal; but I press on to make it my own, because Christ Jesus has made me his own.
text.Rotherham: Not that I have, already, received, or have, already, reached perfection, but I am pressing on—if I may even lay hold of that for which I have also been laid hold of by Christ [Jesus]:—
text.KJV: 3:12 Not as though I had already attained, either were already perfect: but I follow after, if that I may apprehend that for which also I am apprehended of Christ Iesus.
text.DRV: 3:12 Not that now I have received, or now am perfect: but I pursue, if I may comprehend, wherein I am also comprehended of Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: Beloved, I do not consider that I have made it my own; but this one thing I do: forgetting what lies behind and straining forward to what lies ahead,
text.Rotherham: Brethren! I, as to myself, reckon that I have, not yet, laid hold; one thing, however,—the things behind, forgetting, and, unto the things before, eagerly reaching out,
text.KJV: 3:13 Brethren, I count not my selfe to haue apprehended: but this one thing I doe, forgetting those things which are behinde, and reaching forth vnto those things which are before,
text.DRV: 3:13 Brethren, I do not account that I have comprehended. Yet one thing: forgetting the things that are behind, but stretching forth myself to those that are before,

text.NRSV: I press on toward the goal for the prize of the heavenly call of God in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: With the goal in view, I press on for the prize of the upward calling of God in Christ Jesus.
text.KJV: 3:14 I presse toward the marke, for the price of the high calling of God in Christ Iesus.
text.DRV: 3:14 I pursue to the mark, to the prize of the supernal vocation of God in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: Let those of us then who are mature be of the same mind; and if you think differently about anything, this too God will reveal to you.
text.Rotherham: As many, therefore, as are full-grown, let, this, be our resolve; and, if, somewhat differently, ye are resolved, this, also, shall, God, unto you reveal.
text.KJV: 3:15 Let vs therefore, as many as bee perfect, bee thus minded: and if in any thing ye be otherwise minded, God shal reueale euen this vnto you.
text.DRV: 3:15 Let us therefore as many as are perfect, be thus minded: and if you be any otherwise minded, this also God hath revealed to you.

text.NRSV: Only let us hold fast to what we have attained.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, whereunto we have advanced, in the same rank, stepping along.
text.KJV: 3:16 Neuerthelesse, whereto wee haue alreadie attained, let vs walke by the same rule, let vs minde the same thing.
text.DRV: 3:16 Nevertheless whereunto we are come, that we be of the same mind, let us continue in the same rule.

text.NRSV: Brothers and sisters, join in imitating me, and observe those who live according to the example you have in us.
text.Rotherham: Imitators together of me, become ye, brethren, and keep an eye on them who, thus, are walking,—even as ye have, us, for, an ensample.
text.KJV: 3:17 Brethren, be followers together of me, and marke them which walke so, as ye haue vs for an ensample.
text.DRV: 3:17 Be followers of me, brethren, & observe them that walk so as you have our form.

text.NRSV: For many live as enemies of the cross of Christ; I have often told you of them, and now I tell you even with tears.
text.Rotherham: For, many, are walking—of whom I have often been telling you, and, now, even weeping, am telling,—the enemies of the cross of the Christ;
text.KJV: 3:18 (For many walke, of whome I haue told you often, and now tell you euen weeping, that they are the enemies of the crosse of Christ:
text.DRV: 3:18 For many walk whom often I told you of (and now weeping also I tell you) the enemies of the cross of Christ:

text.NRSV: Their end is destruction; their god is the belly; and their glory is in their shame; their minds are set on earthly things.
text.Rotherham: Whose, end, is destruction, whose, God, is the belly, and [whose], glory, is in their shame, who, upon the earthly things, are resolved.
text.KJV: 3:19 Whose end is destruction, whose God is their belly, and whose glorie is in their shame, who minde earthly things.)
text.DRV: 3:19 Whose end is destruction: whose God, is the belly: and their glory in their confusion, which mind worldly things.

text.NRSV: But our citizenship is in heaven, and it is from there that we are expecting a Savior, the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: For, our citizenship, in the heavens, hath its rise; wherefore, a Saviour also, do we ardently await,—The Lord Jesus Christ,—
text.KJV: 3:20 For our conuersation is in heauen, from whence also we looke for the Sauiour, the Lord Iesus Christ:
text.DRV: 3:20 But our conversation is in Heaven: whence also we expect the Saviour, our Lord Jesus Christ,

text.NRSV: He will transform the body of our humiliation that it may be conformed to the body of his glory, by the power that also enables him to make all things subject to himself.
text.Rotherham: Who will transfigure our humbled body, into conformity with his glorified body, according to the energy wherewith he is able even to subdue, unto himself, all things.
text.KJV: 3:21 Who shall change our vile bodie, that it may bee fashioned like vnto his glorious body, according to the working whereby he is able euen to subdue all things vnto himselfe.
text.DRV: 3:21 who will reform the body of our humility, configured to the body of his glory, according to the operation whereby also he is able to subdue all things to himself.

text.NRSV: Therefore, my brothers and sisters, whom I love and long for, my joy and crown, stand firm in the Lord in this way, my beloved.
text.Rotherham: So then, my brethren, beloved and longed for, my joy and crown,—thus, stand fast in the Lord, beloved.
text.KJV: 4:1 Therefore, my brethren, dearely beloued and longed for, my ioy and crowne, so stand fast in the Lord, my dearely beloued.
text.DRV: 4:1 Therefore, my dearest brethren and most desired, my joy and my crown: so stand in our Lord, my dearest.

text.NRSV: I urge Euodia and I urge Syntyche to be of the same mind in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Euodia, I exhort, and, Syntche, I exhort,—on the same thing, to be resolved in the Lord:—
text.KJV: 4:2 I beseech Euodias, and beseech Syntiche, that they be of the same mind in the Lord.
text.DRV: 4:2 Euchodia I desire and Syntyche I beseech to be of one mind in our Lord.

text.NRSV: Yes, and I ask you also, my loyal companion, help these women, for they have struggled beside me in the work of the gospel, together with Clement and the rest of my co-workers, whose names are in the book of life.
text.Rotherham: Yea! I request thee also, true yokefellow, be thou helping together with these women,—who, indeed, in the joyful message, have maintained the combat with me, along with Clement also, and the rest of my fellow-workers, whose names, are in the book of life.
text.KJV: 4:3 And I entreat thee also, true yokefellow, helpe those women which laboured with me in the Gospel, with Clement also, and with other my fellow labourers, whose names are in the booke of life.
text.DRV: 4:3 Yea and I beseech thee my sincere companion, help those women that have laboured with me in the Gospel with Clement, and the rest my coadjutors, whose names are in the book of life.

text.NRSV: Rejoice in the Lord always; again I will say, Rejoice.
text.Rotherham: Rejoice in the Lord always: again, I will say—rejoice!
text.KJV: 4:4 Reioyce in the Lord alway: and againe I say, Reioyce.
text.DRV: 4:4 Rejoice in our Lord always; again I say rejoice.

text.NRSV: Let your gentleness be known to everyone. The Lord is near.
text.Rotherham: Let, your considerateness, be known unto all men. The Lord, is near:—
text.KJV: 4:5 Let your moderation be knowen vnto all men. The Lord is at hand.
text.DRV: 4:5 Let your modesty be known to all men. Our Lord is nigh.

text.NRSV: Do not worry about anything, but in everything by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known to God.
text.Rotherham: For nothing, be anxious, but, in everything, by your prayer and supplication with thanksgiving, let, your petitions, be made known unto God;
text.KJV: 4:6 Bee carefull for nothing: but in euery thing by prayer and supplication with thankesgiuing, let your request be made knowen vnto God.
text.DRV: 4:6 Be nothing careful: but in every thing by prayer & supplication with thanks-giving let your petitions be known with God.

text.NRSV: And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, the peace of God, which riseth above every mind, shall guard your hearts and your thoughts, in Christ Jesus.
text.KJV: 4:7 And the peace of God which passeth all vnderstanding, shall keepe your hearts & minds through Christ Iesus.
text.DRV: 4:7 And the peace of God which passeth all understanding, keep your hearts and intelligences in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: Finally, beloved, whatever is true, whatever is honorable, whatever is just, whatever is pure, whatever is pleasing, whatever is commendable, if there is any excellence and if there is anything worthy of praise, think about these things.
text.Rotherham: For the rest, brethren, whatsoever things are true, whatsoever things are dignified, whatsoever things are righteous, whatsoever things are chaste, whatsoever things are lovely, whatsoever things are of good report,—if there be any virtue, and if any praise, the same, be taking into account;
text.KJV: 4:8 Finally, brethren, whatsoeuer things are true, whatsoeuer things are honest, whatsoeuer things are iust, whatsoeuer things are pure, whatsoeuer things are louely, whatsoeuer things are of good report: if there bee any vertue, and if there bee any praise, thinke on these things:
text.DRV: 4:8 For the rest, brethren, what things soever be true, whatsoever honest, whatsoever just, whatsoever holy, whatsoever amiable, whatsoever of good fame, if there be any virtue, if any praise of discipline, these things think upon.

text.NRSV: Keep on doing the things that you have learned and received and heard and seen in me, and the God of peace will be with you.
text.Rotherham: The things which ye have both learned, and accepted, and heard, and seen in me, the same, practise;—and, the God of peace, shall be with you.
text.KJV: 4:9 Those things which ye haue both learned and receiued, and heard, and seene in mee, doe: and the God of peace shall be with you.
text.DRV: 4:9 Which you have both learned, and received, and heard, & seen in me; these things do ye, and the God of peace shall be with you.

text.NRSV: I rejoice in the Lord greatly that now at last you have revived your concern for me; indeed, you were concerned for me, but had no opportunity to show it.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit I have been made to rejoice in the Lord greatly—that, now, at length, ye have flourished in your care for me,—although, indeed, ye were caring, but lacked opportunity.
text.KJV: 4:10 But I reioyced in the Lorde greatly, that now at the last your care of me hath flourished againe, wherein yee were also carefull, but ye lacked opportunitie.
text.DRV: 4:10 And I rejoiced in our Lord exceedingly, that once at the length you have reflourished to care for me, as you did also care: but you were occupied.

text.NRSV: Not that I am referring to being in need; for I have learned to be content with whatever I have.
text.Rotherham: Not that, as to coming short, I am speaking, for, I, have learned, in whatsoever circumstances I am, to be, independent:
text.KJV: 4:11 Not that I speake in respect of want: for I haue learned in whatsoeuer state I am, therewith to bee content.
text.DRV: 4:11 Ispeak not as it were for penury. For I have learned, to be content with the things that I have.

text.NRSV: I know what it is to have little, and I know what it is to have plenty. In any and all circumstances I have learned the secret of being well-fed and of going hungry, of having plenty and of being in need.
text.Rotherham: I know [what it is] even to be kept low, and I know [what it is] to have more than enough,—in every way, and in all things, have I been let into the secret—both to be well fed, and to be hungering, both to have more than enough, and to be coming short:
text.KJV: 4:12 I know both how to bee abased, and I knowe how to abound: euerie where, and in all things I am instructed, both to bee full, and to bee hungrie, both to abound, and to suffer need.
text.DRV: 4:12 I know both to be brought low, I know also to abound: (every-where, and in all things I am instructed) both to be full, & to be hungry, both to abound, and to suffer penury.

text.NRSV: I can do all things through him who strengthens me.
text.Rotherham: I have might, for all things, in him that empowereth me.
text.KJV: 4:13 I can do all things through Christ, which strengtheneth me.
text.DRV: 4:13 I can all things in him that strengtheneth me.

text.NRSV: In any case, it was kind of you to share my distress.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, nobly, have ye done, in taking fellowship with me in my tribulation.
text.KJV: 4:14 Notwithstanding, yee haue well done, that ye did communicate with my affliction.
text.DRV: 4:14 Nevertheless you have done well, communicating to my tribulation.

text.NRSV: You Philippians indeed know that in the early days of the gospel, when I left Macedonia, no church shared with me in the matter of giving and receiving, except you alone.
text.Rotherham: Ye know, moreover, even ye, Philippians,—that, in the beginning of the glad-message, when I went forth from Macedonia, not, so much as one assembly, with me had fellowship—in the matter of giving and receiving—save, ye alone,—
text.KJV: 4:15 Now ye Philippians know also, that in the beginning of the Gospel, when I departed from Macedonia, no Church communicated with mee, as concerning giuing and receiuing, but ye onely.
text.DRV: 4:15 And you also know, o Philippians, that in the beginning of the Gospel, when I departed from Macedonia, no Church communicated unto me in the account of gift and receit, but you only:

text.NRSV: For even when I was in Thessalonica, you sent me help for my needs more than once.
text.Rotherham: That, even in Thessalonica, both once and again, unto my need, ye sent:—
text.KJV: 4:16 For euen in Thessalonica, ye sent once, and againe vnto my necessitie.
text.DRV: 4:16 For unto Thessalonica also, once and twice you sent to my use.

text.NRSV: Not that I seek the gift, but I seek the profit that accumulates to your account.
text.Rotherham: Not that I seek after the gift, but I seek after the fruit that is to abound unto your account.
text.KJV: 4:17 Not because I desire a gift: but I desire fruit that may abound to your account.
text.DRV: 4:17 Not that I seek the gift, but I seek the fruit abounding in your account.

text.NRSV: I have been paid in full and have more than enough; I am fully satisfied, now that I have received from Epaphroditus the gifts you sent, a fragrant offering, a sacrifice acceptable and pleasing to God.
text.Rotherham: But I have all things in full, and have more than enough, I am filled, having welcomed from Epaphroditus the things that came from you,—a fragrance of sweet smell, an acceptable sacrifice, well pleasing unto God.
text.KJV: 4:18 But I haue all, and abound. I am full, hauing receiued of Epaphroditus the things which were sent from you, an odour of a sweet smell, a sacrifice acceptable, well pleasing to God.
text.DRV: 4:18 But I have all things, and abound: I was filled after I received of Epaphroditus the things that you sent, an odour of sweetness, an acceptable host, pleasing God.

text.NRSV: And my God will fully satisfy every need of yours according to his riches in glory in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, my God, will fill up your every need, according to his riches in glory in Christ Jesus.
text.KJV: 4:19 But my God shall supply all your need, according to his riches in glory, by Christ Iesus.
text.DRV: 4:19 And my God supply all your lack according to his riches in glory, in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: To our God and Father be glory forever and ever. Amen.
text.Rotherham: Now, unto our God and Father, be the glory—unto the ages of ages. Amen!
text.KJV: 4:20 Now vnto God and our Father be glory for euer and euer. Amen.
text.DRV: 4:20 And to God & our Father be glory world without end. Amen.

text.NRSV: Greet every saint in Christ Jesus. The friends who are with me greet you.
text.Rotherham: Salute ye every saint in Christ Jesus. The brethren who are with me, salute you.
text.KJV: 4:21 Salute euery Saint in Christ Iesus: the brethren which are with me, greet you.
text.DRV: 4:21 Salute ye every saint in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: All the saints greet you, especially those of the emperor’s household.
text.Rotherham: All the saints salute you, but especially they who are of Caesar’s household.
text.KJV: 4:22 All the Saints salute you, chiefly they that are of Cesars houshold.
text.DRV: 4:22 The brethren that are with me, salute you. All the saints salute you: but especially they that are of Cæsar's house.

text.NRSV: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
text.Rotherham: The favour of our Lord Jesus Christ, be with your spirit.
text.KJV: 4:23 The grace of our Lord Iesus Christ be with you all. Amen. It was written to the Philippians from Rome, by Epaphroditus.
text.DRV: 4:23 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
